Permanent Baseline — Feb 2021 Coup Structural Floor
⚠ MGRI Permanent Minimum: ~0.28 — Coup Is the Baseline

Unlike standard canaries, the Myanmar CBM Canary operates with a structural geopolitical floor. The February 2021 military coup by the SAC (State Administration Council) created a permanent elevated risk environment that means the MGRI will never return below approximately 0.28 regardless of individual signal states.


This means:

  • Zone thresholds are shifted upward compared to standard CivilisationOS canaries: NORMAL<0.40 (not <0.30), WATCH<0.65 (not <0.60).
  • All five KC scores carry a structural minimum driven by the ongoing coup, international pariah status, and absence of any normalisation pathway.
  • MGRI 0.4800 WATCH is the current stable state — not a temporary spike but the steady-state of coup governance.
  • KC_04 jade at 0.60 is the current highest-risk channel, elevated above the structural baseline by active mining halts and US JADE ACT enforcement.
Feb 2021 SAC Coup
Military seized power 1 Feb 2021. Aung San Suu Kyi detained. International sanctions, NUG/PDF resistance formed. Armed conflict ongoing across all 14 states/regions. No ceasefire or political settlement pathway visible.
ASEAN Five-Point Consensus: Stalled
ASEAN 5-point consensus (2021) unimplemented. No special envoy progress. Myanmar suspended from ASEAN summits but remains member. No multilateral pressure mechanism functioning.
PDF / EAO Territorial Gains
Operation 1027 (Oct 2023) & subsequent EAO offensives captured key towns. SAC territory down from 65% (2021) to ~40% (2026). Conflict intensity at highest since 2021. No SAC military solution visible.

KC_04 JADE highest current risk (0.60): Myanmar produces ~90% of global jade supply. Mining halts and US JADE ACT enforcement create direct TIF (Tiffany) and SIG (Signet) earnings risk. Structural minimum ensures MGRI never falls below ~0.28 even with all signals at minimum.

US Stock Impact | Baseline MGRI 0.4800 | TIF/SIG Bearish · LDOS Bullish
StockTickerMyanmar ExposureDirectionPrimary Risk / Catalyst
Tiffany & Co (LVMH)TIF 🔴 CRITICAL — Jade Sourcing BEARISH ⬇ Myanmar = ~90% global jade supply. Jade disruption + US JADE ACT enforcement = Tiffany sourcing constraint and reputational risk. KC_04 at 0.60 directly triggers TIF exposure.
Signet JewelersSIG 🔴 HIGH — Gemstone Supply BEARISH ⬇ Sanctions escalation (KC_02) + jade disruption (KC_04) = gemstone supply chain constraint. OFAC designation risk for any supplier with Myanmar jade exposure.
KKR & CoKKR 🟡 LOW — ASEAN PE NEUTRAL ASEAN regional risk channel. Myanmar conflict spillover risk to regional PE / infrastructure investments. Largely exited direct Myanmar exposure post-coup.
ChevronCVX 🟡 LOW — Yadana Exit NEUTRAL Exited Yadana pipeline 2022. Residual reputational + legal risk from pre-exit operations. Yadana gas disruption (KC_05) no longer direct Chevron earnings exposure.
Flex LtdFLEX 🔵 MINIMAL — ASEAN Mfg NEUTRAL ASEAN manufacturing exposure. Myanmar not a major Flex hub post-coup. Regional risk monitor only.
Leidos HoldingsLDOS 🟢 BULLISH — Defense Intel BULLISH ⬆ Sanctions escalation (KC_02) and Pacific defense engagement drive US DoD intelligence and surveillance contracts. Myanmar conflict increases US Indo-Pacific defense posture spending. LDOS benefits from escalation channel.
TIF + SIG Dual Jade Kill: Myanmar produces approximately 90% of the world's jadeite jade supply. The combination of US JADE ACT enforcement, active mining halts (KC_04=0.60), and OFAC sanctions on jade mining entities creates a direct sourcing constraint for any jeweller with Myanmar jade in their supply chain. TIF (Tiffany, now LVMH) and SIG (Signet Jewelers) are the primary US market bearish kills in this domain.
LDOS Bullish on Sanctions Channel: Leidos Holdings benefits from the Myanmar geopolitical deterioration through increased US DoD spending on Pacific intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) capabilities. As Myanmar becomes an increasing regional instability vector, US defense engagement in the Indo-Pacific expands. LDOS is the lone bullish name in the CBM Myanmar domain.
Jade & Natural Resource Monitor — KC_04 (Score: 0.60 ELEVATED)
Risk FactorLevelDetailStatus
Myanmar Jade Production HALTED / SHARPLY REDUCED Hpakant jade mining region (Kachin State) disrupted by KIA/conflict. Annual exports collapsed from $3.5B (2018) to $0.6B est. (2026). 🟠 ELEVATED
US JADE Act Enforcement ACTIVE Tom Lantos Block Burmese JADE Act (2008) prohibits import of Myanmar jade and rubies into US. Strengthened post-coup. Supply chain due diligence required. 🔴 ACTIVE ENFORCEMENT
Myanmar Global Jade Share ~90% of global supply Hpakant region (Kachin State) is the world's largest jadeite source by far. Canada and Russia supply remaining ~10%. No substitute supply available at scale. 🔴 DOMINANT CONCENTRATION
Ruby / Gemstone Exports SEVERELY REDUCED Mogok valley rubies (world's finest) also sanctioned. Combined jade + ruby exports near historic lows. 🟡 WATCH
Sanctions Monitor — KC_02 (Score: 0.40 WATCH)
Sanctions AuthorityStatusDetailTrend
US OFAC (Myanmar) 135 entities designated (Jul 2026) SAC leadership, military conglomerates (MEHL, MEC), jade mining companies, financial institutions. New designations follow each major offensive or rights violation. ESCALATING
EU Sanctions (Myanmar) ACTIVE EU sanctions on SAC leadership and military entities. Coordinated with US OFAC rounds. Asset freezes and travel bans. 🟡 ACTIVE
UK Sanctions (Myanmar) ACTIVE UK Global Human Rights Sanctions Regulations. Mirrors US/EU framework. OFSI enforcement. 🟡 ACTIVE
US JADE ACT FULLY ENFORCED Import ban on Myanmar jade and rubies. Post-coup enforcement intensified. Affects TIF, SIG supply chains directly. ENFORCED
SWIFT / Financial Isolation PARTIAL CUT-OFF Major Myanmar banks SWIFT-disconnected. CBM foreign reserves severely constrained. MMK not convertible at official rates. 🔴 SEVERE
Natural Gas / Yadana Pipeline Monitor — KC_05 (Score: 0.50 WATCH)
IndicatorReadingDetailStatus
TotalEnergies Exit EXITED Jan 2022 TotalEnergies withdrew from Yadana and Yetagun gas projects citing human rights conditions. Largest Western exit from Myanmar energy sector post-coup. COMPLETED
Chevron Exit EXITED Oct 2022 Chevron sold Yadana stake after TotalEnergies exit. Gas revenue now flows primarily to PTTEP (Thailand) and Myanmar state entities. COMPLETED
Yadana Gas Revenue (2026 est.) $0.9B Down from $2.1B (2018 peak). Thailand (PTT) is primary buyer via pipeline. Revenue flows to SAC-controlled entities. Declining production + lower prices. 🟡 DECLINING
Thailand Buyer Risk PRESENT PTT/PTTEP continuing to purchase Yadana gas under contract. Secondary sanctions risk if US escalates enforcement. Thailand political balance critical. 🟡 WATCH

Natural gas pipeline revenue is a primary SAC hard-currency income source. Disruption (pipeline sabotage, buyer exit, secondary sanctions on Thailand) would accelerate the KC_03 currency collapse channel by removing the SAC's main FX earner. KC_05 and KC_03 are structurally linked: gas revenue loss = MMK pressure amplification.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.2300  NORMAL

Central Bank of Myanmar operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Strait of Malacca (significant dependency, weight 0.50).

Critical import dependencies include refined fuels, food commodities, industrial machinery, consumer electronics. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Primary exports — natural gas, jade, gemstones, teak — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.2300 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: Strait of Malacca closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

🏢
Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.2500  WATCH

Central Bank of Myanmar is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is moderate CRE exposure with manageable vacancy pressures.

As a frontier market, Myanmar real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

Developer stress is flagged as active. Developer debt overhang and pre-sale structure failures are live risk vectors — similar to the Evergrande cascade in China. This is the highest-weight RERI driver for Myanmar, pushing the composite score materially above baseline.

RERI 0.2500 WATCH — Structural real estate vulnerabilities are present but not acute. Monitor property price indices, mortgage delinquency rates, and CRE vacancy data. Stress could escalate if interest rates remain elevated.

Watch signals: Developer bond defaults or pre-sale refund demands → cascade risk  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

🛡
Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.4200  WATCH

Central Bank of Myanmar faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Hurricane / Typhoon, Flood, Earthquake. High-severity events (Hurricane / Typhoon, Earthquake) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Myanmar is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.4200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Hurricane / Typhoon event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
